codeReview.summary: Current HEAD still does not resolve the round-2 enhanced-path runtime smoothness blocker. I reviewed the fix feature's handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `2be94f8`, then compared `2be94f8..HEAD`. Since round 2, the repo gained shared-state/metadata updates (including the new headed/GPU-capable validation requirement in `frontend-experience-worker/SKILL.md`) plus unrelated tokenomics changes, but there were no relevant implementation changes in `src/app/globals.css`, `src/components/visual/**`, or `.factory/library/tradebetter-comparison-evidence.md`. The latest feature evidence therefore remains the same fallback-only desktop observation from the original rerun, so VAL-VISUAL-025 still lacks a concrete runtime smoothness or jank observation from a session where the enhanced desktop path was actually active.

issues.description: The only recorded desktop runtime evidence for this feature is still the fallback-state observation `data-visual-state=fallback` / `data-motion-layers=0` caused by the headless WebGL limitation. No later implementation or evidence change landed on current HEAD to replace that with a headed/GPU-capable enhanced-path run, so there is still no frame-rate, jank, dropped-frame, long-task, GPU-layer, or equivalent runtime smoothness observation from an actually active enhanced desktop session as required by VAL-VISUAL-025.

summary: Fail. No relevant implementation changes landed after the round-2 review, so the fallback-only desktop evidence from the original rerun still stands unchanged and the enhanced-path runtime smoothness blocker remains unresolved on current HEAD.
